nativeRange.habitat: Reef crests and lagoons; typically found at depths of 6–65 m, most commonly 6–20 m near coral heads

diet.notes: Generally accepts vitamin-enriched brine shrimp, mysis shrimp, and quality prepared carnivore foods. Tends to dart out from cover to snag passing prey items. A varied diet has been noted to help maintain coloration.

summary: A small, cryptic dottyback from Western Pacific reefs, generally considered reef-safe and suitable for nano to mid-sized systems. Males tend to display pinkish-red coloration while females are paler with yellow-green fin gradations.
careNotes: Pseudoplesiops typus is a notoriously secretive species that typically hides within rockwork, occasionally dashing out to snag food. It is generally considered reef-safe and has been reported to accept a variety of prepared and frozen foods in captivity. Best kept singly; have been known to harass smaller, passive species such as nano gobies, particularly in confined spaces. The species displays sexual dichromatism — males tend to be pinkish-red while females are paler pink with yellow-green gradations on the fins. A diet that includes vitamin-enriched foods has been associated with better color retention. NOTE — temperature disagreement: LiveAquaria lists 72–78°F (22.2–25.6°C) while FishBase wild data shows 26.6–29°C; these ranges do not overlap. The averaged value of 24–27.5°C is used here but should be reviewed. The FishBase range likely reflects wild Indo-Pacific reef temperatures more accurately. Tank size: LiveAquaria recommends 10 gallons (38 L), but the minimum nice-value of 75 L is used to allow adequate rockwork and territory.

additional.name: FishBase
additional.notes: Cross-checked adult size (4.6 cm SL), temperature range (26.6–29°C), native range, and taxonomy. Temperature disagreement with LiveAquaria noted — ranges do not overlap; averaged values used and dataStatus set to needs_review.
